case_id: varies (selected via OFFSET 350 and 420 from patient_data table)

pi_items.verified_value: Population Health Coordinator is a real occupation in healthcare, particularly in ACOs and population health management programs
pi_items.source: Web search - verified through job description databases (ZipRecruiter, Glassdoor) and healthcare education resources (AdventHealth University, MPH Online)

pi_items.metadata_value: Population Health Management / Accountable Care Organization
pi_items.verified_value: Both are real industries in healthcare. ACOs are voluntary groups of providers coordinated under CMS rules. Population Health Management is a healthcare strategy used by providers, insurers, and ACOs.
pi_items.source: Web search - verified through CMS, AHA (American Hospital Association), CDC, and healthcare management literature

pi_items.verified_value: File path is created during setup_task.sh - confirmed in script line 103-178 which generates the document dynamically with patient-specific risk data
Ground truth file path is created during setup_task.sh - confirmed in script lines 58-95 which generates JSON with patient data and baseline counts

pi_items.metadata_value: Not in metadata but referenced in scripts as NHANES
pi_items.verified_value: NHANES (National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey) is real CDC/NCHS data. Patients are drawn from the 2011-2012 survey cycle (~9,375 patients) via LibreHealthIO's official NHANES SQL dump at https://github.com/LibreHealthIO/lh-ehr/raw/master/sql/nhanes/libreehr_nhanes.sql.gz
pi_items.source: Code analysis (setup_librehealth.sh line 58-59), Web search verified CDC NHANES as official NIH/CDC survey data, confirmed GitHub repository has the file

pi_items.metadata_value: Not in metadata but claimed in setup as 32% for Patient 1, cited as 'greater than 20 percent' in setup_task.sh line 40
pi_items.verified_value: ASCVD (Atherosclerotic Cardiovascular Disease) 10-year risk score ≥20% is the clinical threshold for 'high risk' per ACC/AHA 2019 Primary Prevention Guideline. Score of 32% exceeds this threshold and indicates high cardiovascular risk requiring statin therapy.
pi_items.source: Web search - verified through ACC (American College of Cardiology), AHA (American Heart Association) 2019 guidelines, MDCalc clinical decision support, and UpToDate

pi_items.metadata_value: Not in metadata but claimed in setup as 11.2% for Patient 2, cited as 'above 9 percent' in setup_task.sh line 41
pi_items.verified_value: HbA1c >9% is the clinical threshold for 'uncontrolled diabetes' or 'poor glycemic control' per HEDIS (Healthcare Effectiveness Data and Information Set) measures. HbA1c of 11.2% exceeds this threshold and indicates inadequate diabetes management.
pi_items.source: Web search - verified through HEDIS measures, eCQI Resource Center, healthcare quality databases, and medical literature

pi_items.metadata_value: Task involves real patient records from NHANES with synthetic risk stratification
pi_items.verified_value: Patient demographics (names, DOB, ethnicity, gender) are real from NHANES 2011-2012 survey. Risk stratification scores (32% ASCVD, 11.2% HbA1c) and clinical risk problem assignments are synthetic - created during setup_task.sh for this specific task instance, not derived from actual patient records.
pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh lines 15-41 showing hardcoded risk values; NHANES data inspection confirming real patient demographics

privileged_info_summary: This task involves real patient data from NHANES (9,375 patients from the 2011-2012 CDC National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey), though a specific pair of patients is selected during task setup. The occupation 'Population Health Coordinator' is a real healthcare role in Accountable Care Organizations and population health management programs. The clinical thresholds referenced (ASCVD 10-year score ≥20% for high cardiovascular risk and HbA1c >9% for uncontrolled diabetes) are authentic evidence-based clinical guidelines from ACC/AHA and HEDIS. However, the specific risk stratification analysis and clinical risk problem assignments for the selected patients are synthetic, created during task setup rather than derived from actual patient records.
